rawCommits.subject: fix(stats): wire token persistence + close cost attribution gaps (#5)
rawCommits.body:
* fix(stats): wire persist_task_output to task_completed/task_failed

Closes a refactor-introduced gap: `persist_task_output` in
`apps/aura-os-server/src/persistence.rs` was dead code with zero
callers, despite its docstring saying it should be called when a task
completes or fails. As a result, tokens accumulated in
`task_output_cache` by `update_usage_cache` on every harness
`assistant_message_end`/`token_usage` event were silently
discarded — never written to `tasks.total_input_tokens` /
`total_output_tokens` in aura-storage. This is the primary cause of
Neo's 'Tokens stat shows 0' report.

Both terminal task-event handlers (`task_completed` and
`task_failed`) now call a new `persist_cached_task_output` helper
that drains the cached entry for the task, hands it to
`persist_task_output`, and removes it from the in-memory map so
memory doesn't grow unbounded across task completions.

`persist_task_output` itself was already correctly built to write
tokens, files_changed, and live output via `update_task` +
`create_event` — only the wiring was missing.

Pairs with a follow-up aura-storage stats SQL change so the
dashboard's "Tokens" stat reads from `tasks` (where tokens now
land) instead of `sessions` (where nothing writes them in the
current architecture).

* fix(stats): stamp aura-* attribution on summary calls + tool sessions

Two flows that previously made router calls without aura-* headers,
causing their cost to be excluded from per-project aggregation:

* generate_session_summary now stamps x-aura-session-id,
  x-aura-project-id, and x-aura-agent-id on the LLM call. Caller
  updated to thread project_id + agent_id from the URL path.

* project_tool_session_config (used by spec-generate and task-extract
  harness sessions) now resolves and populates aura_org_id from the
  project's org. session_id stays None — these are utility tool
  sessions without a live storage session — and the loosened
  aura-router accepts project_id alone for cost attribution.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: wire token persistence + close cost attribution gaps (#5)

rendered.title: Token stats and per-project cost attribution restored
rendered.intro: Today's nightly closes a long-standing gap that left the dashboard's Tokens stat stuck at zero and quietly excluded several LLM round-trips from per-project cost rollups. The fix re-wires task-completion persistence and stamps the missing attribution headers on summary and tool-session calls.

entries.title: Task token usage now persists on completion and failure
entries.summary: A dormant persistence path is finally wired up so token counts and task output stop being silently dropped when tasks finish, and previously unattributed router calls now carry the headers needed for per-project cost aggregation.

items.text: Task completion and failure handlers now drain the in-memory task output cache into aura-storage, so accumulated input/output tokens, files-changed, and live output actually land on the task record instead of being discarded — the root cause behind the dashboard's Tokens stat showing 0.

Session summary generation now stamps x-aura-session-id, x-aura-project-id, and x-aura-agent-id on its router call, threading project and agent IDs from the URL so the resulting token usage is no longer recorded with a null project_id and dropped from per-project cost rollups.

Tool sessions used by spec-generate and task-extract now resolve and attach the project's aura_org_id, and aura-router has been loosened to accept project_id alone, so utility tool calls without a live storage session still get attributed to the right project.

highlights: Dashboard Tokens stat now reflects real task usage
Session summaries and tool sessions count toward per-project cost
